channel.title: Ontology on Junho Lee
channel.description: Recent content in Ontology on Junho Lee

item.title: DataNexus Architecture: Four Layers and Two Flows
item.description: Why ontology-based routing instead of a single NL2SQL pipeline — and what the second flow, from Pull to Push, looks like

item.title: 11. AI answers can be wrong. How should they appear on screen?
item.description: How the DataNexus Chat UI shows answer evidence: SQL, Runtime Trace, fallback state, permission scope, sources, and the knowledge graph alongside the answer.

item.title: 8. 30-Question Retail Sample at 80%: Four PDCA Cycles
item.description: After wiring up the router, I ran a 30-question benchmark and pushed NL2SQL EX (Execution Accuracy) from 66.67% to 80%. Here’s what I fixed across four cycles and where things broke.

item.title: 7. When a Question Comes In, Who Decides the Routing?
item.description: The term definitions are done. But when a user asks a question, who decides whether to search the graph, write SQL, or run a vector search? Things I ran into while designing the router.

item.title: 6. When You Don't Have to Build Agent Infra Yourself, Harnesses Become Obsolete. What About the Ontology?
item.description: Shortly after the Conway leak, Anthropic officially launched Claude Managed Agents. As agent infrastructure gets absorbed into platforms, here’s why DataNexus’s ontology layer remains safe.

item.title: Legal Data Gets a Brain: The Synergy of korean-law-mcp and DataNexus
item.description: korean-law-mcp streamlines legal data access while DataNexus’s knowledge-graph-based ontology layer explicitly maps complex statutory connections, significantly enhancing AI’s ability to interpret law accurately.

item.title: 1. Why We're Building DataNexus
item.description: The structural problems with enterprise data analytics, and why we decided to build an ontology-based data agent platform.
